title: Go Booleans
content:
### Booleans in Go

The `bool` type represents truth values: `true` and `false`.

### Declaration

```go
var isActive bool = true
var isDone bool      // false (zero value)
isReady := false
```

### Boolean Operations

```go
a := true
b := false

fmt.Println(a && b)  // false (AND)
fmt.Println(a || b)  // true  (OR)
fmt.Println(!a)      // false (NOT)
```

### Comparison Results

All comparison operators return a boolean:

```go
x := 10
y := 20

fmt.Println(x == y)  // false
fmt.Println(x != y)  // true
fmt.Println(x < y)   // true
fmt.Println(x > y)   // false
fmt.Println(x <= y)  // true
fmt.Println(x >= y)  // false
```

### Zero Value

The zero value of `bool` is `false`:

```go
var flag bool
fmt.Println(flag) // false
```

### Short-Circuit Evaluation

Go uses short-circuit evaluation for boolean operators:

```go
// If isAdmin is false, the second expression is NOT evaluated
if isAdmin && hasPermission("delete") {
    // delete
}

// If isCached is true, the second expression is NOT evaluated
if isCached || fetchData() {
    // use data
}
```

> **Key Point:** Booleans cannot be converted to/from integers (0/1) in Go. Use explicit comparison instead.
codeExample:
package main

import "fmt"

func main() {
    isSunny := true
    isWarm := false

    fmt.Println("isSunny:", isSunny)
    fmt.Println("isWarm:", isWarm)
    fmt.Println("Go outside:", isSunny && isWarm)
    fmt.Println("Maybe go:", isSunny || isWarm)

    var isReady bool
    fmt.Println("isReady (zero value):", isReady)
}

quiz.question: What is the zero value of a boolean?
